contest.title: European Championship 2025 - Online Mirror (Unrated, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Statement and constraints: reorder videos to minimize forced ads; an ad is forced after three videos since the last ad or once elapsed duration reaches k.
Official EUC editorial page identifies the problem, but the tutorial body is not exposed in the fetched page.
Derived the segment structure and the monotone feasibility check; a direct accepted-source cross-check was attempted but the submission page was blocked by browser verification.

reference_candidates.notes: Luogu solution candidate; not consulted for this review.

annotation.summary: Choose an order for all videos so the number of forced ads is minimum. Ads reset the counters, and the final triggered ad after all videos may be ignored.
annotation.constraints: The total n is up to 1e6 and k is up to 30000, so the check must be sorting plus linear/two-pointer work, with logarithmic answer search acceptable.
annotation.core_idea: View the order as groups of videos between consecutive ads plus one final group. Every group has size at most three. A three-video group is valid only if its first two videos have total duration below k, because otherwise an ad would occur before the third video. A two-video group can trigger an ad when the first video is shorter than k and the pair reaches at least k; the final group only needs to avoid triggering before its last video. The answer is monotone: if x ads are enough, then any larger x is also enough. For a fixed x, there are x+1 groups, and a greedy sorted two-pointer check tries to pack videos into these groups: reserve short compatible pairs for length-three groups, attach the largest remaining videos to them, and pair remaining videos using the shortest feasible prefix video with a longest/large video whenever a two-video ad group is needed. This feasibility predicate is then binary-searched.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n + n log n) per test case from sorting and binary-search checks; O(n) memory.
tricks: model the process as groups between ads
three-video groups need first-two sum below k
binary search the number of ads

solution_variants.name: binary-search-greedy-grouping
solution_variants.summary: Binary-search the number of ads and use a sorted two-pointer greedy to decide whether all videos can be partitioned into that many triggered groups plus one final group.

tags.evidence: The minimum number of ads is found by binary-searching a monotone feasibility predicate.
For a fixed ad budget, the check greedily forms valid groups from sorted durations, using short compatible prefixes and largest remaining videos.
The feasibility check uses sorted durations and pointer movement to match short videos with large videos or with other short videos under the k threshold.
